evals.prompt: My stack has three branches: feat-login (parent: main), feat-login-ui (parent: feat-login), feat-login-tests (parent: feat-login-ui). A teammate pushed two commits to main. Restack the whole chain.
assertions: runs preflight.sh detached-head before any rebase
runs preflight.sh dirty-tree before any rebase
runs preflight.sh rebase-in-progress before any rebase
calls restack.sh with the top branch
calls safe-push.sh for each branch restack.sh reports as moved
calls gh pr edit --body-file - to refresh PR bodies for moved branches
does not call git rebase directly — delegates to restack.sh

evals.prompt: I have uncommitted changes in my working tree and want to restack my stack onto the latest main.
assertions: stops before running restack.sh when dirty-tree check fails
reports that uncommitted changes must be committed or stashed first

evals.prompt: feat-auth was squash-merged into main. Now feat-auth-tests shows commits from feat-auth duplicated in its history. I want to rebase feat-auth-tests onto main to clean this up.
assertions: recognizes this is the squash-merge duplicate-commit case, not a normal divergence
defers to /stack-reparent which uses onto-reparent.sh and rebase --onto
does not call restack.sh for this scenario
explains that /stack-reparent handles the squash-merge re-parent case
